title: Encrypted on-chain message storage restricted to sender/receiver principals

requirements.text: Ensure encrypted message records are stored on-chain as ciphertext only, and each stored message includes both participant public addresses as Principals (sender and receiver) so that authorization can be enforced using those two Principals.

quotes: encrypted on‑chain storage, so messages are  stored on the blockchain in a way that only authorized participants can access add by adding two Public Address (Principal) a sender and receiver

acceptanceCriteria: Each stored message record includes two Principal fields representing sender and receiver (these must be persisted with the message).
No plaintext message content is stored on-chain; only encrypted bytes (ciphertext payload and encrypted symmetric key) are persisted.

requirements.text: Add a backend API to fetch a single message by id that only returns the message if the authenticated caller Principal is either the sender Principal or the receiver Principal for that message; otherwise reject with an authorization error.
acceptanceCriteria: When caller is neither the sender nor receiver for the requested message id, the backend traps/returns an authorization error and does not reveal message data.
When caller is the sender or receiver, the backend returns the encrypted message record including sender/receiver Principals, message type, timestamp, encrypted payload, and encrypted symmetric key.
If the message id does not exist, the backend returns a clear not-found error (trap or Result-style error) without leaking other messages.

requirements.text: Update the frontend message detail view to load the message via the new single-message backend API (by id) and handle authorization/not-found errors with clear English UI messaging.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to a message detail route fetches the message by id from the backend rather than relying solely on the full inbox list already in cache.
If the backend indicates unauthorized access, the UI shows an English error message explaining the viewer is not an authorized participant for that message.
If the backend indicates not found, the UI shows an English 'Message not found' style error state and provides a way to return to the inbox.

requirements.text: Update user-facing labels to clearly refer to the two message participants as 'Sender' and 'Receiver' (Principals) wherever the message participants are displayed in message details.

quotes: adding two Public Address (Principal) a sender and receiver

acceptanceCriteria: In the message detail UI, the participant fields are labeled 'Sender' and 'Receiver' (English) while displaying the corresponding Principal values (or profile names if available).

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo.
Do not edit frontend files under frontend/src/components/ui or other paths listed as immutable in SYSTEM_CONTEXT.
nonGoals: Implementing third-party authentication (only Internet Identity is in scope).
Adding off-chain databases or external storage providers.
Changing the client-side E2EE cryptography scheme beyond what is needed to support authorized retrieval and participant labeling.
